volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 3.3.409 - Section 3.4.493)

sections.label: Section 3.3.409
sections.translation: I ’ troth , he was stirring in this business .

sections.label: Section 3.3.409b
sections.translation: Upon my faith , it really is so , clearly .

sections.label: Section 3.3.410
sections.original: Ne tú habes servom graphicum et quantivis preti ,
sections.translation: You really have a clever servant , and worth any price .

sections.label: Section 3.3.411
sections.original: non carust auro contra . ut ille fidicinam
sections.translation: At his weight in gold he would not be dear .

sections.label: Section 3.3.414
sections.translation: It ’ s wonderful how that could be managed .

sections.label: Section 3.3.414b
sections.translation: He said that you were going to offer a sacrifice at home for your son ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.416
sections.translation: because he had returned safe from Thebes .

sections.label: Section 3.3.416b
sections.translation: He hit upon the right thing .

sections.label: Section 3.3.417
sections.original: Immo ipsus illi dixit conductam esse eam ,
sections.translation: Yes , and he himself told her that she had been hired to assist you here in the sacrifice . He said that you were about to perform it , and that you had a sacrifice at home .

sections.label: Section 3.3.420
sections.original: ego illic me autem sic assimulabam : quasi
sections.translation: But I then made pretence that I was ignorant , as it were , inasmuch as I made myself out half-witted [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.421b
sections.translation: Why yes ; it was right to do so .

sections.label: Section 3.3.422
sections.original: Res magna amici apud forum agitur , ei volo
sections.translation: An important trial of a friend is going on at the Forum ; I want to go as his advocate .

sections.label: Section 3.3.423b
sections.translation: Go , and when you have leisure , return to me mediately .

sections.label: Section 3.3.424b
sections.translation: I ’ ll be here just now .

sections.label: Section 3.3.425
sections.translation: Nothing is there more opportune to man than a friend in need ; without labour of your own , what you want is done nevertheless . If I had commissioned any one upon this business , a less skilful person , and less fitted for this matter , he would have been gulled ; and so , grinning with his white teeth ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.430
sections.translation: my son would have most deservedly laughed at me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.435
sections.original: sed quis hic est quém ego huc advenientem conspicor ,
sections.translation: But who is this I see coming this way , that with his swaggering makes his scarf to be streaming in the wind ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.437
sections.original: Cave praéterbitas ullas aedis , quin roges ,
sections.translation: Take care not to pass by any house without asking where lives the old gentleman , Periphanes of Plothea [*] . Take care that you don ’ t return to me without knowing it .

sections.label: Section 3.4.440
sections.original: Adulescens , si istunc hominem , quem tu quaeritas ,
sections.translation: Young man , if I point out to you the person whom you are in search of , what thanks shall I get of you ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.443
sections.translation: In arms , by the might of war , I ’ ve deserved that all people ought to give me thanks .

sections.label: Section 3.4.444
sections.translation: You haven ’ t found out , young man , a tranquil spot

sections.label: Section 3.4.445
sections.original: ubi tuas virtutes explices , ut postulas .
sections.translation: where to recount your virtues as you wish ; for , if an inferior vaunts his battles to a superior , by his lips they become soiled ; but this Periphanes of Plothea whom you are seeking , I am he , if you want him for anything .

sections.label: Section 3.4.450
sections.original: memorant apud réges armis , arte duellica
sections.translation: among kings in arms , by his skill in war , gained vast wealth ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.451b
sections.translation: Aye , if you were to hear of my achievements , drop ping your hands you would run off home .

sections.label: Section 3.4.453
sections.original: Pol égo magis unum quaero , meas cui praedicem ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m rather in search of one to whom to speak of my own , than of one to be speaking of his to me .

sections.label: Section 3.4.454b
sections.translation: This is not the place for it .

sections.label: Section 3.4.455
sections.original: proin tu alium quaeras cui centones sarcias .
sections.translation: Do you then look out for another person , into whom to stuff your scraps of nonsense [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.431
sections.original: atque haec stultitiast me illi vitio vortere
sections.translation: And yet this is folly , for me to impute that to him as a fault , which I myself used to do in my youth when I was a soldier ; in recounting my battles I used to tear out men ’ s ears by the roots , when I had once begun .

sections.label: Section 3.4.456
sections.original: Animum advorte , ut quod ego ad te advenio intellegas .
sections.translation: Lend your attention , that you may learn what I ’ ve come to you about . I ’ ve heard that you have purchased my mistress .

sections.label: Section 3.4.457b
sections.translation: Heyday ! now at last I know who he is ; the officer whom Epidicus was telling me about a short time since . ( To the OFFICER .) Young man , it is

sections.label: Section 3.4.460
sections.translation: as you say ; I have purchased her .

sections.label: Section 3.4.460b
sections.translation: I want a few words with you , if it is not inconvenient to you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.461b
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I don ’ t know whether it ’ s convenient or not , until perhaps you say what you want .

sections.label: Section 3.4.463
sections.original: Mi illam ut tramittas , argentum accipias : adest .
sections.translation: I want you to transfer her to me , and take the ransom .

sections.label: Section 3.4.464
sections.original: nam quid ego apud te vera parcam proloqui ?
sections.translation: But why should I hesitate to speak out to you ? I wish at once to make her my freed-woman , that she may be my mistress [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.466b
sections.translation: I ’ ll make short work with you ; she was bought for me for fifty minae of silver ; if sixty minae are paid down to me , I ’ ll let the damsel employ your holidays [*] ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.470
sections.original: atque ita profecto , ut eam ex hoc exoneres agro .
sections.translation: and so assuredly so , that , if you like , you may remove her from this country .

sections.label: Section 3.4.471
sections.translation: Is she then purchased by me ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.471b
sections.translation: On those terms you may have her .

sections.label: Section 3.4.472
sections.translation: You have made a good bargain .

sections.label: Section 3.4.472b
sections.translation: Hallo there ! bring out of doors the Music-girl you took in . The harp , too , as well , that was thrown in with her , I ’ ll make you a present of it for nothing .

sections.label: Section 3.4.475
sections.translation: Come , take her , please .

sections.label: Section 3.4.475b
sections.translation: What madness possesses you ? What mystery are you devising for me ? Why don ’ t you order the Music-girl [*] to be brought from in-doors ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.477b
sections.translation: Why , this is the Music-girl . There ’ s no other one here .

sections.label: Section 3.4.478b
sections.translation: You can ’ t impose on me . Why don ’ t you bring out here the Music-girl Acropolistis ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.480
sections.translation: This , I tell you , is she .

sections.label: Section 3.4.480b
sections.original: Non haec inquamst . non novisse me
sections.translation: This , I tell you , is not she . Do you suppose that I can ’ t know my own mistress ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.481b
sections.translation: It was this Music-girl , I tell you , for whom my son was dying with love .

sections.label: Section 3.4.482b
sections.translation: This is not she .

sections.label: Section 3.4.483
sections.translation: How , not she ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.483b
sections.translation: It is not .

sections.label: Section 3.4.483c
sections.translation: Where in the world , then , does , she come from ? For my part , i ’ faith , I certainly paid the money for her .

sections.label: Section 3.4.484b
sections.translation: Foolishly paid , I guess , and a mighty mistake .

sections.label: Section 3.4.485b
sections.translation: Nay , but this is she ; for I sent the servant who is in the habit of attending my son ; he himself this moment purchased the Music-girl .

sections.label: Section 3.4.488
sections.original: Em istíc homo te articulatim concidit , senex ,
sections.translation: Well then , this fellow has cut you up joint by joint , old gentleman , this servant of yours .

sections.label: Section 3.4.489b
sections.translation: How , cut me up ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.489c
sections.translation: Such is my suspicion ;

sections.label: Section 3.4.490
sections.original: nam pro fidicina haec cerva supposita est tibi .
sections.translation: for she has been palmed upon you for that Music-girl . Old gentleman , you ’ ve been bubbled clearly and cleverly . I shall now go seek her wherever she is .

sections.label: Section 3.4.492b
sections.translation: Warrior , farewell !

sections.label: Section 3.4.493
sections.original: euge , euge , Epidice , frugi es , pugnavisti , homo es ,
sections.translation: Bravo , bravo ! Epidicus You ’ re a clever fellow ! You have fought well — you ’ re a man ! you ’ ve wiped my nose when snivelling , worthless fellow that I am !
